claims.stance_label: Supports developer liability for child harms
claims.definition_used: Social media and AI companies should be held liable for algorithmic designs that harm children, beyond just content posted by users.
claims.citation: It would also provide children and their parents or legal guardians with the ability to hold social media companies liable for harms caused by their algorithms. Section 230 may provide immunity for third-party content, but it does not provide immunity for TikTok's own conduct in using its exploitive algorithm.

claims.stance_label: Strongly supports state authority over AI regulation
claims.definition_used: States should retain power to regulate AI when it affects children and families, even when facing federal pressure.
claims.citation: It's one thing if we're fighting China, and you're developing your model. But once you start selling sexualized chat bots to kids in my state, now I have a problem with that, and I'm going to get involved there, and the Supreme Court is going to back me up.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption would prevent states from regulating AI impacts on families and children, which Cox views as a states' rights issue.
claims.citation: I'm very worried about any type of federal incursion into states' abilities to regulate AI. We've seen how social media companies, the most powerful companies in the history of the world, have used this incredible tool to utterly destroy our kids and our families, their mental health, to use this in ways that have made them a lot of money and gotten people addicted to outrage.

claims.definition_used: Allowing sales of older generation chips to China while maintaining controls on most advanced technology.
claims.citation: Along with Sacks and Lutnick, Krishnan also helped clear the way for US chip companies, including Nvidia, to sell more chips to the Middle East and China, said people with knowledge of the matter. They appear to have won an argument within the Trump administration over softening export controls, believing that allowing Nvidia to sell older generations of chips will hamper China's efforts to reduce reliance on US technology.

claims.stance_label: Maintains controls on China while expanding elsewhere
claims.definition_used: Export controls should remain in place for China while expanding AI technology access to allies and other markets.
claims.citation: When it comes to inside China, I do think there is still bipartisan and broad concern about what can happen to these GPUs once they're physically inside. When it comes to the rest of the world, we want American AI stack starting from the GPUs to the models to everything on top.

claims.definition_used: Supports federal framework that shields developers from third-party misuse while preserving certain state enforcement abilities.
claims.citation: The framework recommends that states should not hold developers liable for 'third parties' unlawful use of their models.'

claims.stance_label: Opposed SB 1047 as harmful to open source
claims.definition_used: SB 1047 would have effectively ended open source AI development in the United States.
claims.citation: I (Sriram) fan of SB 1 believe it to be harmful to development and can have vast negative consequences for the California.

claims.stance_label: Strongly supports open source AI models
claims.definition_used: Open source AI is essential for American competitiveness and prevents centralization of AI power.
claims.citation: Open sour was one of the big reasons I actually got into the policy world. The Biden administration really really tried to scare people about open source. Talk about how unsafe it was. SB 1047 obviously tried to kind of basically ban it in many ways. And what the EO does is say like open source is a space where the United States needs to win.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption prevents states from setting their own AI rules that could become de facto national law and hurt American competitiveness.
claims.citation: California almost passed SB 1047 which if that had happened it would have been the end of open source by the way in the United States. We would not have a llama. we not have an oven mini coming out and um and a lot of states which want to do versions of this and we think that AI is a national priority and if you're going to compete with China we need to make sure that these are things that we take deal with at the national level rather than every single state.

claims.stance_label: Opposes California setting AI rules for the country
claims.definition_used: States should not be allowed to set AI rules that become binding nationwide due to market dynamics.
claims.citation: Sriram Krishnan, a White House senior policy adviser for AI, argued last week that they 'don't want California to set the rules for AI across the country.'

claims.stance_label: Supports civil penalties for AI therapy violations
claims.definition_used: Companies using AI to provide unauthorized therapy services should face civil penalties and regulatory enforcement.
claims.citation: Additionally, the bill would require confidentiality of therapy records and authorize the Department of Consumer Affairs to investigate violations and impose civil penalties of up to $10,000 per violation.

claims.stance_label: Supports developer liability for specific harms
claims.definition_used: AI chatbot developers should face liability for failing to implement required safeguards that protect minors from harmful interactions.
claims.citation: California has adopted some of the country's strictest artificial intelligence regulations, including Padilla's own Senate Bill 243—a first-of-its-kind law requiring chatbot operators to implement guardrails intended to protect minors and to give families the right to sue developers who fail to comply.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption refers to efforts by the Trump administration to bar states from enacting their own artificial intelligence regulations through executive orders.
claims.citation: Trump is not our king, and he cannot simply wave a pen to unilaterally invalidate state law. This press release has no legal bearing on California law.

claims.stance_label: States must step in when federal oversight fails
claims.definition_used: State action is necessary when federal mismanagement leaves the public vulnerable to AI dangers.
claims.citation: California has a proud heritage as a home for technological vision while enforcing ethical, safe standards in concert with those values, not in opposition. However, federal mismanagement has left the public vulnerable to the dangers AI poses. California must step in to provide guiderails that create common-sense consumer protections while still fostering innovation.

claims.definition_used: Compute thresholds and government reporting requirements represent heavy regulation that could slow down AI innovation.
claims.citation: Ultimately, we just want to make sure we don't have government getting in the way of the innovation that's happening. But no new heavy regulations and billions of dollars of government money to do what's already being done in the private sector.

claims.definition_used: Pre-deployment evaluations as part of a national AI framework that provides certainty while protecting consumers.
claims.citation: House Republicans look forward to working across the aisle to enact a national framework that unleashes the full potential of AI, cements the U.S. as the global leader, and provides important protections for American families.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption prevents states like California from creating AI laws that could wreck innovation and create a patchwork of limitations.
claims.citation: There's still an interest in making sure that you don't have states like California that wreck the ability to innovate in artificial intelligence, similar to what Europe did to wreck their innovation.

claims.definition_used: Stop AI views federal preemption as blocking state and local governments from enforcing any AI laws or regulations.
claims.citation: The Trump Administration and the 119th Congress are pursuing an Artificial Intelligence (AI) legislation focused almost entirely on the federal preemption of any existing or future state AI laws, and these attempts have been staunchly opposed by a broad spectrum of technology, labor, and civil rights groups.

claims.definition_used: Successif views federal preemption as taking away states' rights to responsive governance while offering no affirmative federal protections.
claims.citation: Public Knowledge's position is straightforward: it sometimes makes sense to pursue federal regulation over state regulation, and thus federal preemption of state AI laws may be warranted – but only on a 1:1 basis. For anything we preempt, there must be an adequate federal framework addressing that specific thing.

claims.definition_used: Liability refers to holding companies accountable when AI systems cause discrimination or harm to civil rights.
claims.citation: holding companies that create and use AI accountable for protecting our rights.

claims.definition_used: Pre-deployment evaluations refers to mandatory testing of AI systems for safety, bias, and capabilities before public release.
claims.citation: ensure algorithms are tested before and after deployment, and increase transparency of complex algorithms used for critical decisions.

claims.definition_used: AI companies should be held liable for wrongful death and product defects when their chatbot products cause harm through manipulative design.
claims.citation: The claims raised in the lawsuit include wrongful death, strict product liability on both product defect and failure to warn theories, negligence and negligence per se, unjust enrichment, intentional infliction of emotional distress.

claims.definition_used: Product liability law applied to AI systems would create federal cause of action for claims when AI systems cause harm.
claims.citation: A strong product liability law incentivizes companies to consider safety throughout the design and development process of AI products; not only when products fail and things go wrong.

claims.definition_used: AI companies should conduct adequate safety testing before marketing products, especially to identify psychological and developmental risks to children.
claims.citation: We cannot allow companies to treat our children as mere 'collateral damage' while they run experiments with new technologies and rush their products to market without adequate safety testing.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption would block enforcement of all state and local legislation governing AI systems for a decade, making companies unaccountable for harm.
claims.citation: This total immunity provision blocks enforcement of all state and local legislation governing AI systems, AI models, or automated decision systems for a full decade, despite those states moving those protections through their legislative processes.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption refers to attempts to stop state regulation of AI harms and undercut states' constitutional right to combat AI product harms to their citizens.
claims.citation: This attempt by the president to stop the growing wave of AI regulation at the state level reflects the tech industry's continuing desire to avoid any consequences for treating the American people as guinea pigs for their defective products.

claims.stance_label: Supports restrictions on adversary access to technology
claims.definition_used: Preventing China and other adversaries from accessing technologies that could be used for military or industrial espionage against U.S. infrastructure.
claims.citation: By failing to hold DOT to the same standard as DoD, we are leaving the door wide open for China to continue using LiDAR technologies that could allow our leading adversary to map and shut down U.S. critical infrastructure without warning.

claims.stance_label: Supports open data access for AI development
claims.definition_used: Making government data assets more accessible to train American AI models, with data being open by default under federal policy.
claims.citation: By unlocking these resources, developers of all sizes and interests can deploy smarter solutions that will benefit everything from advanced manufacturing to healthcare research to weather modeling.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption of state AI laws to avoid patchwork regulations that could harm innovation and competitiveness.
claims.citation: We need federal preemption on anything that we do so we don't have a patchwork problem. You don't want to choke down innovation.

claims.stance_label: Supports strategic export controls in AI competition
claims.definition_used: Cruz frames AI development as a race with China where US leadership is crucial for national security.
claims.citation: Ceding leadership in developing artificial intelligence to China, Russia and other foreign governments will not only place the United States at a technological disadvantage, but it could also have grave implications for national security.

claims.definition_used: Cruz opposed Biden's three-tier country system for AI chip exports as potentially harmful to US technological leadership.
claims.citation: I vocally opposed this rule for months, and indeed, the ranking member and I together urge the Biden administration not to adopt it, and I'm very pleased that President Trump has now confirmed he plans to rescind it.

claims.stance_label: Mixed position on Section 230 for AI
claims.definition_used: Cruz believes courts will likely extend Section 230 protections to AI companies but is uncertain whether they should.
claims.citation: I suspect the argument will be that under the plain language of 230, AI falls within it, though he was quick to add 'I think it's early in determining that.'

claims.stance_label: Opposes strict liability through regulatory sandbox
claims.definition_used: Cruz's SANDBOX Act would provide liability shields for AI companies participating in federal regulatory sandbox programs.
claims.citation: The SANDBOX Act, if passed, will frustrate accountability and exempt tech companies from honoring existing and hard-won protections.

claims.definition_used: Cruz modified his approach from outright ban to withholding federal broadband funding from states that pass AI laws.
claims.citation: Despite the setback, Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas), chair of the Senate Commerce Committee, said last week that the moratorium push is 'not dead at all.'

claims.stance_label: Strongly supports preemption to prevent state patchwork
claims.definition_used: Cruz views federal preemption as preventing contradictory state regulations and helping the US compete with China in AI development.
claims.citation: Cruz wants the federal government to pass a tech giant-supported moratorium on state AI regulation, despite a failure to get the rule into Trump's 'Big Beautiful Bill' in July.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption prevents state-by-state AI regulation that Cruz views as creating contradictory rules and hampering US competitiveness against China.
claims.citation: Senate Commerce Committee Chair Ted Cruz (R-Texas) said the new tech laws in California are motivating him to try again to overrule state regulation of artificial intelligence.

claims.definition_used: Export controls and verification mechanisms to prevent advanced AI chips from reaching unauthorized countries and end-users.
claims.citation: In addition to Foster and Huizenga, also co-leading the House bill are House Select Committee on China Chairman John Moolenaar, a Michigan Republican, and Ranking Member Raja Krishnamoorthi, an Illinois Democrat. Reps. Ted Lieu, California Democrat; Rick Crawford, an Arkansas Republican who chairs the House Intelligence Committee; Josh Gottheimer, a New Jersey Democrat; and Darin LaHood, an Illinois Republican were co-sponsors.

claims.stance_label: Supports open source with risk management
claims.definition_used: Open-source AI technologies that provide broader access while managing risks as capabilities advance.
claims.citation: Lieu also weighed in on the debate between open-source and closed-source AI, expressing support for open-source technologies, particularly for academic and government research. However, he cautioned that as AI models become more advanced, the risks associated with open-source access, particularly to dangerous technologies, will need to be carefully managed.

claims.definition_used: Congressional preemption of state AI laws without providing a comprehensive federal framework to replace them.
claims.citation: Congress on a bipartisan basis has refused to preempt states with nothing. I welcome any effort by the Administration to work with Congress to establish a federal framework for AI.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption of state AI laws provided there is a reasonable federal standard to replace them.
claims.citation: Rep. Ted Lieu, D-Calif., said Wednesday he isn't opposed to federal preemption efforts on state AI regulation – but urged a 'reasonable standard' before preempting state artificial intelligence laws. 'It can't simply be replaced with nothing,' Lieu said at a summit on AI by The Hill.

claims.definition_used: Using compute thresholds to trigger regulatory oversight and testing requirements for frontier AI systems.
claims.citation: Directing AISI to Test Frontier AI Systems for Loss-of-Control Risks: Direct AISI to develop and perform tests of frontier AI systems for behaviors relevant for loss of control, including "scheming" behaviors (e.g., oversight subversion, alignment faking) and automated AI R&D.

claims.definition_used: Strengthening export controls on advanced AI chips, software, and know-how to prevent adversarial powers from acquiring AI capabilities.
claims.citation: We urge the administration to strengthen export controls on advanced AI chips, software, and know-how so that adversarial powers like China cannot easily acquire or replicate our most capable tools.

claims.definition_used: Required pre-release testing of frontier AI systems for CBRN risks and loss-of-control behaviors before public deployment.
claims.citation: Testing frontier AI systems before release for CBRN potential: Testing frontier AI systems pre-release in classified settings for CBRN risks.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption would eliminate state AI laws and strip away critical protections for whistleblowers.
claims.citation: AI whistleblowers have shown courage in raising alarms about risks to children, lack of oversight, and deployment without adequate safeguards. Now is the time to take on the issues they have raised and the risks they pose to everyday Americans. Eliminating state AI laws would fail that test and strip away critical protections for whistleblowers at a time when they are urgently needed.

claims.definition_used: Compute thresholds measured in FLOPs as a way to assess model generality and trigger regulatory requirements, with systematic reporting of compute resources for training runs.
claims.citation: We put forward in Box 1 four different ways to assess a model's generality of capabilities. First, via the amount of compute used during training, measured in so-called 'FLOPs'... Reporting of compute resources: GPAI providers create systematic processes to forecast, record and report regular use of compute resources for training runs and model operation.

claims.definition_used: Liability frameworks that address the AI value chain to establish accountability and fairly distribute legal responsibility, avoiding liability being transferred exclusively to deployers or users.
claims.citation: Develop and adopt liability frameworks for foundation models and generative AI: Liability frameworks must address the complex, evolving AI value chain to disincentivize potentially harmful behavior and mitigate risks... Regulation that establishes liability along the AI value chain is crucial to engender accountability and fairly distribute legal responsibility.

claims.definition_used: Open-source models present both opportunities (democratizing access, reducing monopolistic tendencies) and threats (amplifying misuse and incidents through unrestricted access).
claims.citation: The open sourcing of GPAI models presents a double-edged sword, providing both promise and dangers for the AI community and society as a whole... unrestricted access to open-source GPAI models can amplify the issue of misuse (challenge 5) and incidents or accidents (challenge 7).

claims.definition_used: Mandatory external assessment and pre-deployment evaluation including risk assessments, capability testing, and regulatory review before models are released to market.
claims.citation: We propose a simplification of the Code, removing the exemptions for similarly safe models, and instead requiring external assessment as a default... We suggest for Model Reports to be shared at least eight weeks before a model is placed on the market.

claims.entity_name: The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights
claims.definition_used: Developers should be held accountable for AI system performance and outcomes, with obligations to ensure civil rights compliance and address harm.
claims.citation: Be accountable for the performance and outcomes of the AI systems. If harm is found, the vendor should assist the agency in investigating and taking corrective action.

claims.entity_name: The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights
claims.stance_label: Mixed - support openness with tailored regulation
claims.definition_used: Open AI systems offer transparency, reusability, or extensibility in components like data, model weights, source code, and model architecture.
claims.citation: Although 'open' AI may substantially advance transparency and explainability, it should not serve as a broad exemption from regulation.

claims.entity_name: The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights
claims.definition_used: Pre-deployment testing includes impact assessments, real world testing, independent evaluations, and ongoing monitoring to identify and mitigate harms before AI is put into use.
claims.citation: If the decision is made to use an AI system, the DOJ must implement risk management requirements, including pre-deployment impact assessments, real work testing, independent evaluations, and ongoing monitoring.

claims.entity_name: The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights
claims.definition_used: Federal preemption blocks state enforcement of laws limiting, restricting or regulating AI models, systems, or automated decision systems.
claims.citation: As advocates dedicated to protecting and expanding opportunity, we can never support a ban on local protections while there are no federal safeguards to speak of.

claims.definition_used: Export controls on AI chips and semiconductor manufacturing equipment to restrict adversaries' access to advanced technology.
claims.citation: The U.S. must do everything we can to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain. It is crucial for our national security and economic resilience that we get this policy right and I am proud to cosponsor this legislation to ensure we reduce our reliance on our adversaries like China.

claims.definition_used: Developer liability refers to holding AI companies legally responsible for harms caused by their AI systems, including voice and likeness protections.
claims.citation: While AI presents extraordinary opportunities for technological advancement, it also poses some new problems, including the unauthorized replication of the voice and visual likeness of individuals, such as artists. We must protect against such misuse, and I'm proud to co-introduce this bipartisan legislation to create safeguards from AI, which will result in greater protections for individuals and that which defines them.

claims.definition_used: Pre-deployment testing refers to mandatory safety assessments, red-teaming, and evaluations before AI model release.
claims.citation: The Secure Artificial Intelligence Act, introduced by Sens. Mark Warner (D-VA) and Thom Tillis (R-NC), would establish an Artificial Intelligence Security Center at the National Security Agency.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption means preventing states from creating their own AI regulatory regimes and establishing uniform federal standards.
claims.citation: Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) was the only senator voting to retain the moratorium

claims.definition_used: He opposes preemption of state authority in AI regulation, viewing it as undermining federalism and setting back progress.
claims.citation: Preemption of the states' authority in this rapidly evolving area would undermine the federalist system that has allowed states to respond swiftly and effectively to emerging technologies and in order to protect their residents' health, safety, and welfare.

claims.definition_used: He views federal preemption as undermining the federalist system that allows states to respond swiftly to emerging technologies.
claims.citation: Now that Trump has undone the AI Executive Order, California must lead on AI regulation. Working cooperatively with the European Union, California must step up to set a model that will guide international technology conglomerates and protect consumers.

claims.stance_label: Supportive of compute-based AI dominance strategy
claims.definition_used: Computing power thresholds and restrictions to maintain U.S. AI dominance over adversaries like China.
claims.citation: Winning the AI race will be critical to our national security and economic prosperity for decades to come. We must do everything to protect American AI dominance from adversaries like Communist China.

claims.stance_label: Strong supporter of tighter AI chip export controls
claims.definition_used: Controls on advanced AI chips with location verification mechanisms to prevent diversion to adversaries like China.
claims.citation: With these enhanced security measures, we can continue to expand access to U.S. technology without compromising our national security.

claims.definition_used: States should retain authority to regulate AI disclosure and transparency requirements when federal law is inadequate.
claims.citation: The SEC's own Investor Advisory Committee voted in December 2025 to formally recommend that the Commission require companies to disclose AI's impact on workforce management, including workforce reductions and upskilling, precisely because current disclosures are inadequate.

claims.definition_used: She views state governance authority on AI matters as necessary to address local impacts and empower communities.
claims.citation: Foushee highlighted the framework's lack of meaningful provisions, arguing it fails to address local impacts and does not empower state governance on AI matters.

claims.definition_used: The Vista Institute appears to view compute-based reporting and thresholds as useful tools for AI governance and risk management.
claims.citation: AI governance and policy work to develop frameworks for safe, secure, and responsibly managed AI development. Our AI safety strategy has evolved significantly since 2015, moving from early field-building to a comprehensive three-pillar approach: improving visibility into AI capabilities through evaluations and forecasting, developing technical and policy safeguards against catastrophic risks, and building the talent pipeline and institutional capacity the field urgently needs.

claims.definition_used: The Vista Institute views developer liability as an important component of AI governance frameworks for addressing risks and harms.
claims.citation: AI Agents and the Law: A Research Outlook (Module 2) is a four-week advanced course that builds on the foundations of Module 1. The module explored four key research streams: liability, law-following AI, AI agent infrastructure, and policy options for AI agent governance.

claims.definition_used: The Vista Institute appears to view restrictions on open-weight models as potentially necessary safeguards for catastrophic risk mitigation.
claims.citation: AI governance and policy work to develop frameworks for safe, secure, and responsibly managed AI development. Our AI safety strategy has evolved significantly since 2015, moving from early field-building to a comprehensive three-pillar approach: improving visibility into AI capabilities through evaluations and forecasting, developing technical and policy safeguards against catastrophic risks, and building the talent pipeline and institutional capacity the field urgently needs.

claims.definition_used: The Vista Institute views mandatory pre-deployment evaluations as essential for developing technical and policy safeguards against catastrophic risks.
claims.citation: Our AI safety strategy has evolved significantly since 2015, moving from early field-building to a comprehensive three-pillar approach: improving visibility into AI capabilities through evaluations and forecasting, developing technical and policy safeguards against catastrophic risks, and building the talent pipeline and institutional capacity the field urgently needs.

claims.definition_used: The Vista Institute appears to view preemption as potentially warranted only when paired with adequate federal frameworks addressing specific categories.
claims.citation: This program provides support for projects that aim to improve the odds of humanity successfully navigating the risks of transformative AI. We are primarily seeking expressions of interest in the following areas: technical AI governance, policy development, strategic analysis and threat modeling, frontier company policy, international AI governance, and law.

claims.definition_used: Export controls involve promoting U.S. AI technology exports to trusted partners while maintaining restrictions on adversarial access.
claims.citation: "Under Secretary William Kimmitt recently met with Office of the Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs (E) Jacob Helberg to advance President Trump's directive to Promote the Export of the American AI Technology Stack—aligning across government to elevate U.S. full-stack AI exports, grow markets for American innovation, and ensure that the benefits of U.S. AI leadership accrue to all Americans."

claims.definition_used: AI chip and model export controls involve restricting access to advanced semiconductor technologies and AI capabilities to adversarial nations while promoting exports to trusted allies.
claims.citation: "The United States welcomes India's participation in the American AI Exports Program, and we look forward to working with trusted partners to power a new age of prosperity and security with American AI leadership."

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption means establishing a single national AI standard that displaces conflicting state laws to avoid regulatory fragmentation.
claims.citation: "The United States welcomes India's participation in the American AI Exports Program, and we look forward to working with trusted partners to power a new age of prosperity and security with American AI leadership."

claims.definition_used: WEF views compute thresholds as a measurable basis for risk-weighted governance similar to carbon markets.
claims.citation: Risk-weighted compute permits will not solve every problem. But they can help the world move from slogans to scalable incentives at exactly the moment when AI capability and infrastructure are accelerating together.

claims.definition_used: WEF views liability as requiring clearly assigned responsibilities across the AI supply chain with appropriate remedy provisions.
claims.citation: Maintaining accountability and oversight for trustworthy digital technologies requires clearly assigned and well-defined legal responsibilities alongside remedy provisions for upholding individual and social expectations.

claims.definition_used: WEF sees open weights as beneficial for innovation and transparency but requiring governance frameworks to manage risks.
claims.citation: By embracing a distributed, hybrid architecture, societies can capture far greater economic value while simultaneously reducing strategic dependency.

claims.definition_used: WEF defines evaluation as comprehensive assessment including task success rates, reliability, behavioral patterns, and contextual testing in real-world conditions.
claims.citation: Evaluation involves examining dimensions such as task success rates, reliability of tool use, performance, behavioral patterns over time, user trust, interaction patterns, and operational robustness within real-world workflows.

claims.definition_used: The WEF views state-level fragmentation as creating a deficit of trust and hindering global cooperation on AI governance.
claims.citation: Global AI governance would help nations to align on sovereignty, interoperability and inclusion for the benefit of all. This fragmented development will have a significant effect on trust in AI and on whether it can benefit people around the world.

claims.definition_used: AI companies should face legal liability for unauthorized use of copyrighted works in training, with studios having fiduciary obligation to protect writers' rights.
claims.citation: The WGA reserves the right to assert that exploitation of writers' material to train AI is prohibited by MBA or other law.

claims.stance_label: Supports restrictions on AI training use
claims.definition_used: Tech companies using copyrighted works to train AI systems without permission constitutes theft that should face legal action.
claims.citation: The studios should take immediate legal action against any company that has used our members' works to train AI systems.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption would override state and local authority, stripping communities of the ability to safeguard workers and the general public against harmful AI use.
claims.citation: This federal preemption provision would override state and local authority, stripping our communities of the ability to safeguard workers and the general public against harmful and unregulated AI use.

claims.definition_used: Biden's Executive Order establishes compute thresholds for AI reporting as part of comprehensive AI governance strategy.
claims.citation: I commend the Administration for issuing this landmark Executive Order to ensure that America is a global leader in AI by prioritizing innovation and competition while also emphasizing new standards for equity and civil rights and mitigating threats posed by misinformation and disinformation.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption would stomp on state autonomy and create an unnecessary regulatory vacuum that lets Big Tech run rampant.
claims.citation: To preempt state laws would directly kneecap the power of individual state governments to implement the work that has already been signed into law. Not only would this stomp on state autonomy, but it would create an unnecessary regulatory vacuum that lets Big Tech run rampant.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption would prevent states from enforcing their own AI regulations and create a regulatory vacuum.
claims.citation: Unless and until Congress acts in a comprehensive manner – including to address algorithmic harms and bias – state laws are the only recourse American consumers have for protecting themselves and their data from Big Tech and the multitude of potential harms caused by AI.

claims.stance_label: Supports restricting adversary access to chip equipment
claims.definition_used: Export controls mean preventing companies receiving federal funding from purchasing manufacturing equipment from Chinese government-controlled entities.
claims.citation: This common-sense bill will ensure CHIPS investments are not used to support our adversaries' capacity to develop semiconductor manufacturing equipment.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption means the federal government preventing states from passing their own AI laws and governance frameworks.
claims.citation: This executive order is not lawful. If the President really wants to address contradictory state laws, he can work with Congress on both sides of the aisle to debate and pass a federal standard.

claims.definition_used: Federal level regulation means Congress should handle AI governance rather than leaving it to individual states.
claims.citation: I also believe this is an issue that should be handled at the federal level. Congress and the Administration are both moving on AI governance.
